title: Ehrendorferia ochroleuca
intro: Ehrendorferia ochroleuca, also known as golden Ragwort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ehrendorferia ochroleuca include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Ehrendorferia ochroleuca is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. It is native to Afghanistan and Pakistan. The plant's classification places it in the genus Ehrendorferia, which is a group of plants that are often confused with the genus Meconopsis.
